analysis.root_cause: The method generate_form_html() mixes multiple responsibilities—data extraction/validation, template/customization retrieval, HTML string construction, captcha generation wiring, and per-field rendering—into one large procedural flow, causing both complexity and excessive method size.
analysis.explanation: generate_form_html() performs many distinct tasks in sequence: (1) derives title/description from $atts and $form, (2) extracts and validates field configuration from nested form_data, (3) generates captcha data, (4) queries the database for template and customization, (5) builds CSS style strings for multiple customization options (including button styles), (6) assembles the full HTML structure including nonce/hidden inputs, (7) iterates and renders fields, and (8) appends captcha UI and submit/message markup. This results in a long method with many branches and string concatenations, making it harder to read, test, and modify independently. Any change to captcha behavior, template/customization rules, or HTML structure forces edits to the same method, increasing coupling and complexity.

refactor.reasoning: This refactoring splits the large generate_form_html() method into small private helpers for field extraction, style building, and HTML section rendering. It keeps behavior intact while reducing method size, improving readability, and isolating responsibilities so captcha, template/customization, and markup changes can be maintained independently.

new_risks: Potential undefined index risk: build_form_style/build_button_style assume $customization is an array; if database returns null/non-array, behavior may differ (original checked !empty and isset, but refactor passes $customization directly).
Potential undefined index risk: render_form_header uses $form_title/$form_description derived from $atts; same as original, but helper extraction could make future changes easier to break.
validation.comments: The refactor applies Extract Method and meaningfully reduces the complexity/size of generate_form_html by moving field retrieval, style building, and section rendering into helpers. Behavior appears preserved: same HTML structure, same escaping/kses usage, same captcha/nonce/hidden inputs, and same conditional rendering. Minor risk exists around assumptions about the type/shape of $customization returned from the database.

selection.policy: best iteration: preserves_behavior (yes > uncertain > no), then higher improvement_score, then lower attempt number
